message:
feat(web): add WebGL glyph atlas, WASM entry point, and test logging

Three significant additions to the web rendering pipeline:

crates/frankenterm-web/src/glyph_atlas.rs (+436 lines):
  Implements a GPU-backed glyph cache for the WebGL terminal renderer.
  Uses a texture atlas with LRU eviction to rasterize and cache glyph
  bitmaps, avoiding per-frame text layout overhead. Supports subpixel
  positioning and dynamic atlas resizing when the glyph set exceeds
  the initial allocation.

crates/ftui-web/src/lib.rs (+102 lines):
  Adds the WASM entry point and JavaScript interop layer for running
  FrankenTUI in the browser. Wires up the WebGL rendering backend,
  keyboard/mouse event translation from DOM events, and the
  requestAnimationFrame-driven render loop.

crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/test_logging.rs (+190 lines):
  Adds structured test logging infrastructure for the demo showcase,
  enabling detailed per-test timing, assertion counts, and failure
  artifact capture for CI debugging.

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl with current tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2ky9","title":"E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging","description":"# E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging

## Background
Plan Section Testing + Verification requires end-to-end tests that validate the complete pipeline from user input to terminal output. The user specifically requested "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed logging."

## E2E Test Philosophy
E2E tests should:
1. Test the complete system as a user would experience it
2. Run in a PTY to capture real terminal behavior
3. Log every step for post-mortem debugging
4. Be deterministic and reproducible
5. Cover happy paths and error cases

## E2E Test Script Structure

### Directory Layout
```
tests/
  e2e/
    fixtures/          # Test input files
    expected/          # Expected output snapshots
    scripts/           # Test runner scripts
      run_all.sh       # Master test runner
      test_inline.sh   # Inline mode tests
      test_altscreen.sh # Alt-screen tests
      test_cleanup.sh  # Cleanup/panic tests
      test_input.sh    # Input handling tests
    lib/
      common.sh        # Shared utilities
      logging.sh       # Logging functions
      pty.sh           # PTY spawning helpers
```

### Logging Framework (logging.sh)
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive logging for E2E tests

LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-DEBUG}
LOG_FILE=${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/ftui_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log}

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    local caller="${BASH_SOURCE[2]}:${BASH_LINENO[1]}"
    echo "[$timestamp] [$level] [$caller] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_debug() { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" == "DEBUG" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$@"; }
log_info()  { log "INFO" "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN" "$@"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "$@"; }

log_test_start() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "=========================================="
    log_info "STARTING TEST: $test_name"
    log_info "=========================================="
}

log_test_pass() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "✓ PASSED: $test_name"
}

log_test_fail() {
    local test_name=$1
    local reason=$2
    log_error "✗ FAILED: $test_name"
    log_error "  Reason: $reason"
    log_error "  Log file: $LOG_FILE"
}

log_hex_dump() {
    local label=$1
    local data=$2
    log_debug "$label (hex): $(echo -n "$data" | xxd -p | tr -d n)"
}
```

## E2E Test Categories

### 1. Inline Mode Tests (test_inline.sh)
Tests that streaming logs + UI chrome works correctly.

```bash
test_inline_log_scroll() {
    log_test_start "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    
    # Setup
    local pty_output=$(mktemp)
    log_debug "PTY output file: $pty_output"
    
    # Run ftui app that streams 100 log lines with 6-line UI
    log_info "Spawning ftui inline mode app..."
    timeout 10s script -q -c "./target/debug/ftui-harness --mode inline --ui-height 6 --log-count 100" "$pty_output"
    local exit_code=$?
    log_debug "Exit code: $exit_code"
    
    # Verify
    log_info "Verifying output..."
    
    # Check scrollback preservation
    if grep -q "Log line 1" "$pty_output"; then
        log_debug "Early log lines found in scrollback ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "Early log lines missing from scrollback"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check UI region intact
    if tail -6 "$pty_output" | grep -q "\[Status\]"; then
        log_debug "UI region present at bottom ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "UI region not at expected position"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_test_pass "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    rm "$pty_output"
}
```

### 2. Cleanup Tests (test_cleanup.sh)
Tests that terminal is restored after exit/panic.

```bash
test_panic_cleanup() {
    log_test_start "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
    
    # Capture terminal state before
    local before_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state before: $before_state"
    
    # Run app that panics
    log_info "Running app that will panic..."
    ./target/debug/ftui-panic-test 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Capture terminal state after
    local after_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state after: $after_state"
    
    # Verify restoration
    if [[ "$before_state" == "$after_state" ]]; then
        log_debug "Terminal state restored ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Panic cleanup" "Terminal state not restored"
        log_error "  Before: $before_state"
        log_error "  After:  $after_state"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check cursor visible (SGR sequence check)
    log_info "Checking cursor visibility..."
    # ... additional checks ...
    
    log_test_pass "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
}
```

### 3. Input Tests (test_input.sh)
Tests for keyboard, mouse, paste handling.

### 4. ANSI Output Tests
Verify presenter output matches expected sequences.

## Test Output Format

Each test run produces:
1. Console output (PASS/FAIL per test)
2. Detailed log file with timestamps
3. JSON summary for CI
4. PTY captures for failed tests

### JSON Summary Format
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-31T23:45:00Z",
  "total": 50,
  "passed": 48,
  "failed": 2,
  "skipped": 0,
  "duration_ms": 12345,
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_inline_log_scroll",
      "status": "passed",
      "duration_ms": 234,
      "log_lines": 45
    },
    {
      "name": "test_panic_cleanup",
      "status": "failed",
      "duration_ms": 567,
      "error": "Terminal state not restored",
      "log_file": "/tmp/ftui_e2e_20260131_234500.log"
    }
  ]
}
```

## E2E Test Inventory

| Test | Description | Priority |
|------|-------------|----------|
| inline_basic | Basic inline mode display | P0 |
| inline_log_scroll | Log scrolling preserves scrollback | P0 |
| inline_resize | Resize re-anchors UI | P0 |
| inline_cursor | Cursor position contract | P0 |
| altscreen_enter_exit | Alt-screen lifecycle | P0 |
| cleanup_normal | Terminal restored on exit | P0 |
| cleanup_panic | Terminal restored on panic | P0 |
| cleanup_io_error | Terminal restored on IO error | P1 |
| input_key_basic | Basic key parsing | P0 |
| input_key_modifiers | Modifier key detection | P1 |
| input_mouse_click | Mouse click handling | P1 |
| input_mouse_scroll | Mouse scroll handling | P1 |
| input_paste | Bracketed paste handling | P1 |
| input_focus | Focus events | P2 |
| ansi_sgr | SGR sequence output | P0 |
| ansi_osc8 | OSC 8 hyperlink output | P1 |
| ansi_sync | Synchronized output | P1 |
| unicode_basic | ASCII width handling | P0 |
| unicode_wide | CJK wide chars | P0 |
| unicode_emoji | Emoji rendering | P0 |
| unicode_zwj | ZWJ sequences | P1 |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] run_all.sh executes all E2E tests
- [ ] Each test has detailed logging
- [ ] Failed tests produce actionable diagnostics
- [ ] JSON summary generated for CI
- [ ] PTY captures preserved on failure
- [ ] Tests run in < 60 seconds total
- [ ] All P0 tests must pass for v1

## Dependencies
- Requires: bd-2kj (Agent Harness exists to test)
- Requires: bd-10i.11.2 (PTY framework)
- Blocks: bd-2gx (Quality Gates)

## CI Integration
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on P0 test failures
- Upload log artifacts on failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:22:54.577032739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441537371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441432504Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E scripts + harness env hooks; full run passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-2kj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E suite: 31 tests, 6 suites, all passing in 39s. Added test_ansi.sh, test_unicode.sh, inline_resize, inline_cursor_contract.","created_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2lus","title":"Demo Showcase: Input Macro Recorder + Scenario Runner","description":"## Background
A macro recorder turns the demo into a reproducible, scripted showcase. It should record input events, play them back deterministically, and support curated scenarios.

## Scope / Goals
- Record input events with timestamps
- Play back with normalized timing
- Scenario runner for guided demos
- UI to view timeline and replay markers

## Tests / Logging
- Unit tests for record/playback and timing normalization
- Snapshot tests for recorder UI states
- Determinism regression tests
- PTY E2E scripts with verbose logs and captured event traces

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Record + replay produces identical UI output
- [ ] Scenarios run without drift
- [ ] All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Input Macro Recorder + Scenario Runner
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:09.809528710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:49:58.644231063Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:49:58.644213009Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed (bd-2lus.1-9 all closed)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","demo","ux"],"comments":[{"id":374,"issue_id":"bd-2lus","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- FrankenTUI already has input macro utilities. A UI layer and scenario runner would be a standout demo.

Goals
- Record, edit, and replay input macros from the UI.
- Provide a guided demo mode that plays back scenarios reliably.
- Allow snapshot capture at key frames.

Testing
- Unit tests for recorder/playback logic and timing normalization.
- PTY E2E tests that record and replay with verbose event logs.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2lus.1","title":"Macro Recorder spec + UX flow","description":"## UX / Behavior Spec
- Controls: Record / Stop / Play / Loop / Speed
- Timeline view with markers (start/end, key events)
- Failure behavior: if playback fails, show error state + log
- Scenario runner: list of curated macros with descriptions

## Timing Model
- Normalize timing so playback is stable across machines
- Optional fixed‑step playback for deterministic tests

## Tests (Required)
- Unit tests for control state machine
- Snapshot tests for timeline states
- PTY E2E: record → stop → replay

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear control flow and error states
- [ ] Deterministic playback mode specified
- [ ] Test matrix included

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec defines macro record/playback flow, scope (global vs screen), and safety rules.
- Includes unit-test plan for serialization, replay determinism, and input timing normalization.
- Includes E2E test plan that records a macro and replays it with JSONL logs (event stream, timing deltas, success/failure).
- Logging schema specifies macro id, event count, replay drift, and outcome fields.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:10.107631276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:36:44.883647377Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:36:44.883623783Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","design","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lus.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2lus","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Ensure the ftui crates required for demos compile for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`.

Scope
- Remove / cfg-gate: signal handling, raw mode, threads, std::process usage.
- Ensure deterministic time sources can be injected.

Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:35:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","title":"Implement: WASM step-based Program runner (no blocking poll, no threads)","description":"Goal
- Implement a deterministic, JS-driven runtime loop for WASM.

Requirements
- Inputs are delivered from JS (keyboard/mouse/resize/ticks).
- Rendering produces either ftui-render Buffer diffs or FrankenTerm patches.
- No `std::thread` assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can drive an ftui Model with: init -> input -> view -> present cycles.
- Deterministic record/replay possible.

StepProgram in ftui-web provides host-driven, non-blocking Model runner with: init/step lifecycle, DeterministicClock integration, WebBackend buffer capture, BufferDiff computation, tick handling, synchronous task execution. 22 new tests all passing. Also fixed BufferDiff::compute error in ftui-runtime WasmRunner. All quality gates green (fmt, clippy, tests, wasm32 compilation).","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:29Z"},{"id":1017,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WASM step-based runner complete (8bedf73e). Two complementary implementations: (1) ftui-runtime/wasm_runner.rs - platform-agnostic WasmRunner<M> with step/render/push_event API, Buffer+BufferDiff output, 16 tests; (2) ftui-web/step_program.rs - web-specific StepProgram<M> using WebBackend with deterministic clock + presenter integration, 22 tests. Both compile for wasm32-unknown-unknown. Acceptance criteria met: init->input->view->present cycles work, deterministic record/replay proven via tests.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","title":"Integrate: ftui-render Buffer -> FrankenTerm patch feed","description":"Goal
- Connect ftui-render output to FrankenTerm so the browser renderer can present ftui frames efficiently.

Options
- Convert ftui-render Buffer diffs into FrankenTerm patch representation.
- Or define a shared patch protocol used by both.

Acceptance Criteria
- No full-frame copies on steady-state updates.
- End-to-end demo updates are visibly smooth.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","title":"Demo: run ftui-demo-showcase in browser via ftui-web + FrankenTerm","description":"Goal
- Run `ftui-demo-showcase` fully client-side in the browser via `ftui-web` + `frankenterm-web` (no xterm.js).

Scope
- Boot, render, navigate, resize, and respond to keyboard + mouse with low latency.
- Any OS-dependent features must either:
  - have explicit web implementations, OR
  - be explicitly disabled with user-visible UX (no silent partial failure).
- Perf + stability: no leaks, no jank, and predictable frame-time under typical demo workloads.

Testing + Logging (Non-negotiable)
- Create a browser "demo sweep" analogous to `tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh`:
  - emits detailed JSONL (screen_id, seed, size, DPR, timings, frame/patch hashes)
  - failures emit minimal repro traces that can be replayed
- Determinism: recorded sessions replay to identical hashes on the same build.

Acceptance Criteria
- A single web page can run the demo for 10+ minutes without runaway memory or degraded responsiveness.
- JSONL logs validate against the shared schema and are rich enough to debug mismatches.
- Seed/time-freeze determinism is preserved end-to-end.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyDeer","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T09:50:06.745072728Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:14:06.377151045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:33.483325596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:14:06.710181461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1046,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WindyDeer progress slice for `bd-lff4p.3.6` (integration lane): patch-run extraction in `ftui-web` so browser hosts can forward incremental updates into `frankenterm-web` `applyPatch` without full-frame copies.

Implemented
- `crates/ftui-web/src/lib.rs`
  - Added patch payload model:
    - `WebPatchCell { bg, fg, glyph, attrs }`
    - `WebPatchRun { offset, cells }`
    - `WebPatchStats { dirty_cells, patch_count, bytes_uploaded }`
  - Extended `WebOutputs` with:
    - `last_patches: Vec<WebPatchRun>`
    - `last_patch_stats: Option<WebPatchStats>`
  - `WebPresenter::present_ui` now derives deterministic patch runs from `(Buffer, BufferDiff, full_repaint_hint)` and records aggregate upload stats.
  - Added deterministic full-buffer + diff coalescing helpers in `ftui-web` (row-major contiguous spans, grapheme fallback glyph behavior aligned with current web patch pipeline).
  - Added presenter tests covering:
    - full repaint stats/path
    - incremental diff coalescing across runs.

- `crates/ftui-web/src/step_program.rs`
  - Extended tests to assert first-frame/full-repaint patch stats and incremental patch stats are captured.

- `crates/ftui-web/tests/wasm_step_program.rs`
  - Added wasm-side assertion that rendered steps expose patch runs + stats.

Validation
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check -p ftui-web --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy -p ftui-web --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p ftui-web`
- `cargo check -p ftui-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`

Note on workspace gate
- `cargo check --all-targets` is currently failing due unrelated concurrent edits in `frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_fixtures.rs` (constructor/field mismatch), outside this slice.

Why this matters for `.3.6`
- This lands the missing low-level bridge so a browser host can take `ftui-web` step outputs and feed only changed spans into `frankenterm-web` efficiently and deterministically.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:52:45Z"},{"id":1050,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CalmPrairie progress slice (2026-02-09): added browser-style sweep determinism + JSONL gate in crates/ftui-demo-showcase/tests/golden_trace_corpus.rs using ftui-web StepProgram over AppModel.
